records.query: What does the operator manual of the mir 250 say about emergency stops?
records.final_answer:
I found comprehensive information about emergency stops in the MIR 250 operator manual. Let me provide a summary of the key findings.

---

## Emergency Stop Information from MIR 250 Operator Manual

According to the PDF documentation in the MIR 250 type shell's HandoverDocumentation submodel, here's what the manual says about emergency stops:

### **Important Warning**
> **NOTICE:** Only press the Emergency stop buttons in emergency situations. They are not designed for frequent use. ([Manual p. 85](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=85))

### **Emergency Stop Circuit**
The Emergency stop circuit goes through the Auxiliary emergency stop interface and connects to the top module. Any number of Emergency stop buttons can be connected to the circuit. When the circuit is broken, the robot goes into Emergency stop. ([Manual p. 86](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=86))

### **Shared Emergency Stop**
The MIR 250 has a shared emergency stop function that enables the robot and top module to trigger each other into an Emergency stop. ([Manual p. 87](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=87))

**How it works** ([Manual p. 97](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=97)):
- **Outputs**: Signal to the top module when the robot is in Emergency stop (24V when operational, 0V when in E-stop)
- **Inputs**: Allow the top module to bring the robot into Emergency stop (24V = can operate, 0V = E-stop triggered)
- If pins are unequally set for more than 3 seconds, the safety PLC registers an error and the robot must be restarted

### **Safety Interface Pins**
The Auxiliary safety function interfaces (A and B) include:
- **Shared emergency stop in** (Input): Inactive = robot goes into emergency stop
- **Shared emergency stop out** (Output): Inactive when the robot is in emergency stop
- **Safeguarded stop** inputs for Protective stop functionality

([Manual p. 205](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=205))

### **Safety Training Requirement**
Personnel operating near the robot must be informed on how to engage the robot's Emergency stop function in emergency situations. ([Manual p. 36](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=36))

records.final_answer:
According to the MiR250 operator manual (PDF documentation), here's what it says about emergency stops:

## Emergency Stop Function

**Basic Operation** ([Manual p. 84](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=84)):
- The robot enters Emergency stop when an Emergency stop button is pressed physically
- Internal safety contactors switch off, cutting power to the robot's top application and all moving parts (audible clicks can be heard)
- Status lights turn red; the robot cannot move or accept missions
- **To resume**: Release the Emergency stop button, then press the flashing blue Resume button

**Important Notice** ([Manual p. 85](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=85)):

- The Emergency stop circuit goes through the Auxiliary emergency stop interface
- Any number of Emergency stop buttons can be connected to the circuit
- When the circuit is broken, the robot goes into Emergency stop
- Uses two output pins (24V) and two input pins; both inputs must receive 24V for operation
- If input signals are unequal for more than 3 seconds, the robot enters Protective stop until fixed

## Shared Emergency Stop ([Manual p. 87](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=87), [p. 97](http://localhost:8091/submodels/dXJuOnN1Ym1vZGVsOm1pcjI1MDp0eXBlOmhhbmRvdmVyZG9jdW1lbnRhdGlvbg/submodel-elements/Documents%5B0%5D.DocumentVersions%5B0%5D.DigitalFiles%5B0%5D/attachment#page=97)):
